domain:
In the context of a theater organization, various plays are performed in one or more theaters, each identified by its own location, for example "The Northwest Theater". A play may have multiple scheduled sessions, and each session occurs on a scheduled date. Plays are identified by a unique name and is either a musical or an opera. Furthermore, each play may take place during the day or at night.

Each play is written by one or more authors, who are uniquely identified by name. Plays involve participants each also identified by name, and the same participant may act in multiple plays. For each play session, the starting time and an ending time is recorded, and participants can act in one or more sessions. Both authors and participant can be the same person, who may write a play and participate in its performance.

questions.question:
In the context of a author participant, is it always associated with at least one play, or can it exist without any?
For example:
1. An author participant can exist without any play.
2. An author participant must have at least one play.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: An author participant can exist without any play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An author participant must have at least one play.
questions.selected: Option 1: An author participant can exist without any play.

questions.question:
In the context of an author participant, can there be more than one play involved, or is it always associated with just one? 
For example:
1. An author participant is linked to a single play.
2. An author participant is linked to multiple plays.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Each author participant is always linked to a single play.
questions.option_2: Option 2: An author participant can be linked to multiple plays.
questions.selected: Option 2: An author participant can be linked to multiple plays.

questions.question:
Should name be modeled as a separate class associated with Theater, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Name is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual name properties.
2. Name is modeled as a simple representation in Theater. This is suitable when name details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: We need the name details separately.
questions.option_2: Option 2: A single line of text for name is enough.
questions.selected: Option 2: The name is a simple representation in Theater.

questions.question:
Should the name in theater be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use name as string attribute in theater, which allows any text value.
2. Use name as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: northwest, southeast, northeast or southwest.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The name can be any text value.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The name must be one of these: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: The name can be any text value.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: The name can be any text value.

The domain description indicates that theaters are identified by their location, suggesting that the name can be any text value that users want to enter, rather than being limited to specific predefined values.
